page: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, providing 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs. The memory subsystem consists of 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for matrix math acceleration.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in text processing and single-threaded decompression, with Geekbench text processing and small-model LLM text generation reaching top-tier percentiles. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptography, physics simulations, and static web serving throughput. However, memory bandwidth benchmarks for large uncached blocks fall into the average range, and the system exhibits weak performance during text generation tasks with large 70B parameter language models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.8xlarge provides high memory density and DDR5 speeds, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that require substantial RAM without the overhead of GPU hardware.

The hardware profile and benchmark outcomes make this instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, including database operations, caching layers, enterprise applications, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in text processing and small-model text generation, alongside strong results in compression and cryptography. However, text generation for large 70B parameter models remains a bottleneck. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon processor, and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.9 GHz.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and Intel Xeon processors for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores via Intel Xeon 6975P-C at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications
